content: We can reframe curiosity-driven autonomous agent design around a tiered "episodic curiosity memory" architecture: high-priority unexpected observations (selected by intrinsic curiosity modules like ICM) are retained in fast, retrievable long-term memory, while low-novelty redundant observations are pruned during active learning cycles to reduce memory bloat. This creates a self-sustaining active learning loop where the agent's curiosity directly shapes its own memory organization to improve future learning efficiency.
